cards.back: A popular partitional clustering algorithm that groups data points into K clusters based on their mean distance from a centroid.
A density-based clustering algorithm that groups data points into clusters based on their density and proximity to each other.

cards.front: What is Grid-based Approach in Clustering?
cards.back: A method of clustering where the data space is divided into a grid, and data points are assigned to clusters based on their grid cell.

cards.front: What is the main difference between DBSCAN and K-Means clustering?
cards.back: DBSCAN is a density-based algorithm, whereas K-Means is a partitional algorithm. DBSCAN groups data points based on density, whereas K-Means groups data points based on their mean distance from a centroid.

cards.front: What is the main advantage of DBSCAN clustering?
cards.back: DBSCAN can handle noise and outliers in the data, and can discover clusters of varying densities.
